claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch and router system implements brownfield PRD readiness with cross_check evidence policy and read-only evidence agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier).

evidence.summary: Evidence dispatch runtime builds plans, generates agent prompts, executes read-only provider agents, and normalizes results into DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA.
evidence.why: Core evidence dispatch implementation shows read-only execution policy and agent prompt structure.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false and result_schema: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA.
evidence.why: Agent protocol definitions confirm read-only nature and required evidence structure.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandTriage classifies brownfield vs greenfield demands, assigns evidence_policy (cross_check for technical/high-cost risks), and determines route (careful vs fast).
evidence.why: Triage logic shows when evidence agents are required and why cross_check policy applies.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness checks required slots, blockers, assumptions, evidence results, and agent completion before declaring prd_ready.
evidence.why: PRD readiness inspection validates evidence agents must complete before proceeding.

evidence.summary: Codex provider sets ai.codex_sandbox = 'read-only' and ai.codex_approval = 'never' for evidence agents.
evidence.why: Enforces read-only execution for Codex provider in evidence context.

evidence.summary: Custom provider sets ai.custom_sandbox = 'read-only' for evidence agents.
evidence.why: Enforces read-only execution for custom provider in evidence context.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan returns execution_policy with default_mode: 'dry_run', writes_business_code: false, agent_instruction: 'read_only'.
evidence.why: Dispatch plan explicitly defines read-only, dry-run default execution policy.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates agent instructions with 'Hard rules: READ ONLY. Do not edit, create, delete, or format project files.'
evidence.why: Agent prompt explicitly instructs read-only behavior at the agent level.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime checks executeAgents AND allowAgentDispatch before executing; otherwise returns dry_run or blocked status.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement of explicit authorization prevents accidental evidence agent execution.

evidence.summary: Tests verify buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan returns dry_run default and runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime requires explicit authorization.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms security model and authorization requirements.

evidence.summary: Tests verify explorer, cross-checker, and verifier execute in sequence and feed results into readiness inspection.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms cross-check flow and integration with PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Tests verify agent conflicts (e.g., explorer says 'proceed', cross-checker says 'block') result in readiness blockers and prd_ready: false.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms conflict detection prevents unverified PRD promotion.
Shows CLI integration point for evidence dispatch functionality.

evidence.summary: outputDirFor places evidence results in .yolo/demand/evidence/<dispatch-id>/ with role-specific JSON outputs.
evidence.why: Shows evidence output location and file organization.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult converts provider output into evidence_result schema with confidence, evidence, assumptions, risks, missing, and recommendation fields.
evidence.why: Shows result normalization and required evidence structure.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceTasks creates explorer task for single_agent policy, adds cross-checker and verifier for cross_check policy.
evidence.why: Shows task generation based on evidence policy from triage.

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement detects conflicts when evidence agents disagree on claims (e.g., proceed vs block recommendations).
evidence.why: Shows conflict detection logic that prevents averaging away disagreements.

assumptions: Evidence agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) are invoked via provider adapter (spawnProviderPrompt) but actual provider implementation (Claude, Codex, custom) is not examined in this review.
Evidence dispatch assumes provider agents respect the read-only instruction; no enforcement at the provider level is visible in these files.
The demand session state (.yolo/demand/<id>/session.json) structure is assumed to match the schema implied by loadSession and buildStatusSession functions.
Git operations (readBaseCommit) in runtime.ts are assumed to succeed in git repositories; fallback to '0000000' for non-git contexts is assumed safe.
risks: If evidence dispatch is bypassed or agents ignore read-only instructions, PRD facts could be generated from unverified assumptions, leading to incorrect brownfield schema/API changes.
If provider agents do not respect the 'READ ONLY' hard rule in the prompt, they could modify project files during evidence gathering.
If conflicts are averaged instead of blocked, contradictory evidence could incorrectly advance PRD readiness.
missing: Actual usage logs or state files showing evidence dispatch has been run in a real project context.
Provider adapter implementation (spawnProviderPrompt from runtime/execution/provider-adapter.js) is not examined here.
Demand session state examples from .yolo/demand/ directories are not examined in this review.
Integration tests showing full end-to-end evidence dispatch with real provider agents (not just mocks).

result.notes: Evidence dispatch and router implementation confirms brownfield PRD readiness with cross_check policy, read-only agent execution, and conflict detection. Missing evidence about actual usage, provider implementation, and end-to-end integration justifies cross-check verification.
